exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing matchInArray utility in mux.go provides exactly the dedup check needed. Instead of append(allMethods, methods...), iterate methods and only append if not already in allMethods.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is minimal and uses the existing matchInArray utility; no new data structures needed.

alternatives: Use a map[string]struct{} for O(1) dedup
key_decisions.decision: Use matchInArray (O(n)) since the number of methods is always small
key_decisions.reasoning: Consistent with the existing codebase style; matchInArray is already used in the same file; method sets are small (typically 2-8 methods)

solve_notes: In getAllMethodsForRoute, replaced unconditional `append(allMethods, methods...)` with a loop that uses matchInArray to skip methods already in allMethods. This prevents duplicates when multiple routes with the same path register the same HTTP methods.
task_text:
Fix CORSMethodMiddleware producing duplicate methods in the Access-Control-Allow-Methods header

In `middleware.go`, `getAllMethodsForRoute` collects methods from all
routes that match the request path, but when multiple routes with
overlapping path matchers register the same HTTP methods, the collected
list contains duplicates (e.g., `GET,GET,POST`). The
`Access-Control-Allow-Methods` header is then set with repeated values.
Fix `getAllMethodsForRoute` to deduplicate the methods list before
returning.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: matchInArray
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: existing utility for checking slice membership; using it prevents writing a new dedup helper

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just getAllMethodsForRoute. The thrash_preventing set adds matchInArray because an AI agent would search for an existing dedup utility before writing one from scratch. Knowing matchInArray exists and its signature upfront avoids that search.
